title: MEHO Helm chart values
description: Typed contract for the MEHO Helm chart. Helm validates `.Values` against this schema on `helm install` / `helm upgrade` / `helm template` / `helm lint` (Helm uses JSON Schema draft-07). `additionalProperties: false` is set at every object level so typos and stale keys fail loudly with a clear path. Required-but-blank fields use `minLength: 1` to reject the safe-by-default empty placeholders shipped in `values.yaml` — operators MUST override them via `--set` or a values overlay.

replicaCount.description: Number of backplane replicas. v0.1 is single-replica; HA lands in v0.2.

repository.description: OCI image repository (host + path). Pattern matches the lowercase / hyphen / underscore / dot / slash grammar OCI image references use, with an optional `:<port>` segment after the host for private registries (e.g. `registry.example.com:5000/team/meho`).

tag.description: Image tag. Empty fails the schema — Goal #11 deploy discipline requires every install to pin an immutable tag. The pattern matches the OCI tag grammar (`[A-Za-z0-9_][A-Za-z0-9_.-]{0,127}`) so a forgotten `<REPLACE: ...>` placeholder from an example values file fails at install instead of producing an image reference Kubernetes rejects at Pod create.

podSecurityContext.description: Pod-level securityContext. Schema deliberately allows any shape because the Kubernetes PodSecurityContext surface is wide and partially version-dependent; the chart's defaults are pinned but operators may add fields the cluster admits.

securityContext.description: Container-level securityContext. Same rationale as podSecurityContext.

host.description: Hostname the Ingress publishes. Required and shape-validated (non-empty + RFC 1123 hostname) when ingress.enabled is true — enforced by the allOf below. When ingress.enabled is false the template skips the Ingress resource entirely and host may be left blank.

secretName.description: Kubernetes Secret name holding the TLS certificate. Required (non-empty) when both ingress.enabled and ingress.tls.enabled are true — enforced by the allOf below.

keycloakCliClientId.description: Public OAuth client_id surfaced to `meho login` via `GET /api/v1/auth-config` as `cli_client_id`. The CLI maps this field (NOT `keycloakAudience`) to the device-code `client_id`. Must be a Keycloak public client (no client secret) with the device-grant flow enabled and an audience mapper that injects `keycloakAudience` into the issued access token. Default `""` keeps backwards compatibility with v0.3.1 — the backplane endpoint still returns the field but the CLI surfaces an actionable public-client error rather than silently misusing `keycloakAudience` (which is the confidential resource-server id and is rejected by Keycloak's device-grant endpoint with `401 unauthorized_client`). G0.9.1-T9, RDC dogfood Signal #16, 2026-05-21. See `deploy/values-examples/README.md` for the realm-side recipe.

retrievalEmbeddingModel.description: fastembed-supported model identifier (G0.4-T2 #259). Default `BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5` (384-dim, English). Swapping to a model with different output dimensionality requires a re-embed-everything migration because `documents.embedding` is hard-pinned to `vector(384)` by migration 0003.

retrievalModelCacheDir.description: Absolute filesystem path fastembed reads cached ONNX weights from. Default `/opt/meho/model-cache` is the image layer the shipped default model is baked into at build time (evoila/meho#574) — offline + version-locked first boot, no PVC needed. Repoint to a writable `retrieval.modelCache.mountPath` (and set `retrieval.modelCache.enabled: true`) only when overriding `config.retrievalEmbeddingModel` to a non-default model fetched at runtime; the chart does not cross-validate the two values to keep override flexibility for operators that mount via `extraVolumes`.

backplaneUrl.description: MCP audience binding (G0.8-T4 #633). Public base URL of the backplane, no trailing slash. Leave blank for the common ingress-fronted deploy: the chart derives `https://<ingress.host>` (scheme follows `ingress.tls.enabled`). Set explicitly when the public URL differs from the Ingress host. Must be empty or an `http(s)://` URL.

mcpResourceUri.description: MCP audience binding (G0.8-T4 #633). Canonical MCP resource URI, no trailing slash. Leave blank to derive `${BACKPLANE_URL}/mcp` (the documented default). Set explicitly only for a non-default MCP mount point. Must match the Keycloak oidc-audience-mapper value — see `docs/cross-repo/mcp-client-setup.md` Step 1. Must be empty or an `http(s)://` URL.

forwardedAllowIps.description: Comma-separated IPs / CIDRs uvicorn trusts as upstream proxies for `X-Forwarded-Proto` / `X-Forwarded-For` parsing (rendered into the `FORWARDED_ALLOW_IPS` env var; uvicorn reads it natively). Fixes the HTTPS→HTTP redirect downgrade behind a TLS-terminating Ingress (Issue #730, Signal #3, RDC dogfood 2026-05-20). Default `127.0.0.1` matches uvicorn's secure default and fails-closed in-cluster (the Ingress controller's pod IP is never the loopback). Operators MUST override for production with the Ingress controller's pod CIDR (e.g. `10.42.0.0/16` for the RKE2 default). `*` disables the allow-list and is acceptable only for ephemeral CI / single-tenant labs where every connecting peer is the cluster's own proxy. See `docs/cross-repo/reverse-proxy-contract.md`.

credentialsSecret.description: Kubernetes Secret name holding the DATABASE_URL value at key `url` — required.

memory.description: G5.2 memory-layer knobs. `userDefaultTtlDays` drives the default TTL injection G5.2-T2 (#624) applies on `POST /api/v1/memory`. `expiryTickIntervalSeconds` and `expiryEnabled` gate the in-process memory-expiry sweeper started by the FastAPI lifespan (G5.2-T1 #623); set `expiryEnabled: false` when an external cleanup mechanism (k8s CronJob etc.) reaps expired rows instead. The Pydantic validators in `backend/src/meho_backplane/settings.py` enforce the same bounds at app startup; the schema here surfaces them at `helm install` / `helm template` time so misconfigured values fail before the pod ever starts.

userDefaultTtlDays.description: Default time-to-live for user-scoped memory entries, in days. Range [1, 365]; default 7 matches consumer-needs.md §G5.

expiryTickIntervalSeconds.description: Cadence of the in-process memory-expiry sweeper, in seconds. Range [60, 86400]; default 86400 (24 h) matches Initiative #374.

expiryEnabled.description: Whether to start the in-process memory-expiry sweeper. Set false when an external cleanup mechanism reaps expired rows.

topology.description: G9.3-T6 (#858) topology-history retention knobs. The retention prune (`historyPruneEnabled` gated) is the only place the application deletes from the G9.3-T1 (#856) `graph_node_history` / `graph_edge_history` tables -- the tables are otherwise append-only. `historyRetentionDays: 0` is the keep-forever opt-out sentinel (the loop still runs as a logged heartbeat but every tick is a no-op); `historyPruneEnabled: false` skips starting the loop entirely. The Pydantic validators in `backend/src/meho_backplane/settings.py` enforce the same bounds at app startup.

historyRetentionDays.description: Maximum age (in days) of `graph_node_history` / `graph_edge_history` rows the prune preserves. Range [0, 3650]; 0 is the keep-forever opt-out sentinel; default 90 matches Initiative #365 work-item #8.

historyPruneIntervalSeconds.description: Cadence of the in-process retention prune sweeper, in seconds. Range [60, 604800]; default 604800 (7d / weekly) matches Initiative #365's stated cadence.

historyPruneEnabled.description: Whether to start the in-process retention prune sweeper. Set false when an external retention mechanism reaps rows instead. Distinct from `historyRetentionDays: 0` (which keeps the loop running but every tick is a no-op heartbeat).

broadcast.description: Activity-broadcast subchart values (Valkey 9.x per ADR 0005). When `broadcast.enabled` is false the subchart resources are skipped entirely; the rest of this block may be left at defaults in that mode. When enabled, the subchart's own values.schema.json (charts/broadcast/values.yaml shape) is also enforced — this block declares the top-level surface visible to the parent chart.

replicas.description: v0.1 caps the broadcast subchart at one replica — no Sentinel sidecar ships in this chart and streams are ephemeral by design. HA via Sentinel/Cluster lands at v0.2+ (ADR 0005). Raising this above 1 in v0.1 would not produce a working HA topology and is rejected by the schema.

global.description: Helm injects the parent chart's `.Values.global` into every subchart's values namespace. The key must be permitted by the schema; the contents are arbitrary (operators use it to pass shared values across subcharts).

migrationJob.description: Pre-install / pre-upgrade migration Job (Helm hook) running `python -m meho_backplane.db.migrate` before the Deployment rolls forward.

backoffLimit.description: Number of Kubernetes-side retries on non-zero exit. Bounded at 10 to keep the hook latency predictable; raise via override only when a known-transient upstream dependency demands it.

ttlSecondsAfterFinished.description: Seconds after Job completion before Kubernetes garbage-collects the Job. Capped at 24h — longer retention is the operator's responsibility via `kubectl get jobs -o yaml > backup` before TTL fires.

eso.description: Optional chart-side External Secrets Operator (ESO) resource rendering. The default `enabled: false` keeps the chart decoupled from the secret-sync mechanism — Goal #11's cross-repo convention is that the consumer's GitOps repo owns the ExternalSecret manifests. Flipping `enabled: true` renders ExternalSecret CRs for `postgres.credentialsSecret` and (when `eso.keycloak.enabled: true`) the Keycloak client secret, both referencing the named SecretStore. The chart never renders the SecretStore / ClusterSecretStore itself — the consumer installs ESO and creates the store before applying this chart. `eso.secretStore.name` and `eso.postgres.remoteKey` are allowed empty in the default-disabled mode; the conditional `allOf` below enforces non-empty values + the ESO API shape only when `eso.enabled: true`, so a misconfigured opt-in still fails at install time.

name.description: Name of the existing SecretStore / ClusterSecretStore the rendered ExternalSecret references. Required (non-empty) when `eso.enabled: true`.

kind.description: Kind of the referenced store. ClusterSecretStore is the typical choice on RKE2 clusters because the store lives once cluster-wide; SecretStore is namespace-scoped.

remoteKey.description: Vault KV path that holds the DATABASE_URL value. Empty → the template derives it as `<vault.paths.kv>/postgres` at render time.

remoteProperty.description: Property within the remote KV entry that holds the DATABASE_URL string. Defaults to `url` to match the chart's Deployment env, which reads the synced Secret's `url` key.

keycloak.description: Optional ExternalSecret for the Keycloak client secret. v0.1 does not yet consume this Secret in the Deployment env — it is rendered so the consumer's CI can verify the sync end-to-end before the v0.2 Keycloak federation wiring lands. Target Secret name is `<release>-keycloak-client`, keyed `client_secret`.

ingressControllerNamespace.description: Namespace label value selecting the ingress controller. Must match RFC 1123 label syntax.

postgresCIDR.description: PostgreSQL egress CIDR (IPv4). Required and shape-validated (non-empty IPv4 CIDR) when networkPolicy.enabled is true — the rendered NetworkPolicy refuses to ship a wide subnet by accident. When networkPolicy.enabled is false the template skips the NetworkPolicy resource entirely and this field may be left blank.

vaultCIDR.description: Vault egress CIDR (IPv4). Same conditional shape rules as postgresCIDR.

keycloakCIDR.description: Keycloak egress CIDR (IPv4). Same conditional shape rules as postgresCIDR.

affinity.description: Kubernetes Affinity surface — wide and version-dependent; schema permits arbitrary shape.

extraVolumes.description: Extra Kubernetes Volume objects to attach to the backplane Pod AND the migration Job Pod. Each item is a full Volume shape; the chart renders the list verbatim. Loose `items: object` rather than enumerating every Volume source type because Kubernetes adds new ones over time and the API server validates downstream. Most common use: trust-manager CA bundle ConfigMaps (Issue #209).

extraVolumeMounts.description: Extra Kubernetes VolumeMount objects for the backplane container AND the migration Job container. Pair each entry with an `extraVolumes` item of matching name — a mount with no matching Volume fails Pod admission.

extraEnv.description: Extra Kubernetes EnvVar objects (value or valueFrom) appended to the backplane container env AND the migration Job container env. Most common use: pointing `SSL_CERT_FILE` at a mounted trust-bundle path so Python's default ssl context trusts internal-CA-signed Vault / Keycloak / Postgres endpoints (Issue #209). Don't collide with the chart's own keys (DATABASE_URL, BROADCAST_REDIS_URL).

retrieval.description: Retrieval substrate (G0.4, Initiative #225) chart-side surface. v0.2 ships only the fastembed model cache PVC; future G0.4 work (reranker model cache, embedding-config rollout knobs) extends this block.

modelCache.description: fastembed ONNX-weight cache PVC. When `enabled: true` the chart renders a PVC named `<release>-fastembed-cache` and mounts it on the backplane Pod at `mountPath`. When `false` the chart skips both the PVC and the mount; fastembed downloads weights to an ephemeral location on every pod restart.

size.description: Kubernetes resource quantity. Default `200Mi` fits `BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5` (~120 MB on disk) with headroom; raise for `bge-base` (~450 MB) or other larger models.

mountPath.description: Absolute container path. Must match `config.retrievalModelCacheDir` for fastembed to see the cache contents.

storageClassName.description: Empty = cluster default StorageClass. Set explicitly when the cluster has multiple classes and the default is not appropriate for a small rarely-written cache volume.
